english: Lt.; _dégs-kál ('degs khal) 'a bushel by weight' Cs., or rather: twenty points on the large steelyard. — *jug-tág* (jug tag) W. water-wagtail. — *dég-k'a* ('deg kha) C., W., weight. — _dégs-dpon ('degs dpon) is said to denote a military dignity, but is not generally known; as 'servant waiting at table', it ought to be spelled stégs-dpon (stegs dpon). — _dégs-šiṅ ('degs shing) Sch. yoke, fitted to a person's shoulders, for carrying water-buckets etc.
pf. deṅ (deng), imp. deṅ(s) (deng(s)), to go, esp. p'yir déṅ-ba (phyir 'deng ba) to go back, to return, Dzl., Lex. Cf. _doṅ-ba ('dong ba).

pf. and imp. ded (ded), sometimes preceded by rjés-su (rjes su), to go or walk behind, hence 1. to drive, cattle, the herdsman walking behind the animals, whereas of the shepherd _k'rid-pa ('khrid pa) is used; rluṅ-gis gru _ded (rlung gis gru ded) the wind drives the ship, frq.; also to drive through (a tube) by blowing, to blow through Glr.; to drive (animals, birds) from a place of rest, to rouse, start. — 2. to pursue, chase, run after, rgód-ma _déd-pa (rgod ma 'ded pa) to be in the rut (of a stallion); *ded táṅ-če* (ded tang ce) W. to chase, to hunt; *déd-de bó-če* (ded de bo ce) W. to call after a person. — 3. vb. n. to follow in succession, to succeed, rím-pa bžiṅ (rim pa bzhin) successively, of generations, Glr. — 4. to call in, to recover, money, debts; bú-loṅ-_ded drág-po (bu long 'ded drag po) a severe dun Mil.; _déd-mi ('ded mi) a driver, e.g. the person walking behind the horse of a rider, driving it on Lt.; the pursuer of a fugitive Glr. — Cf. bdá-ba (bda' ba).

pf. btab (btab), fut. ytab (gdab), imp. t'ob (thob), supine _débs-su ('debs su), and _dáb-tu ('dab tu), W. *táb-če* (btab ce), imp. *tob* (thob); to cast, throw, strike, hit, variously applied, cf. rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa), in B. gen. with instr., even if there is a dative in the same sentence, v. the examples; *čóg-tse-la táb-če* (lcog tse la btab ce) W. to strike upon the table; rluṅ-gis (rlung gis), gdóṅ-gyis (gdong gyis), nád-kyis _débs-pa (nad kyis 'debs pa), to be beaten by the wind, to be possessed by a demon, to be seized with an illness, frq.; sṅágs-kyis _débs-pa (sngags kyis 'debs pa) B., mtu btáb-pa (mthu btab pa) col., to pronounce a charm against a person or thing, with la (la); lan (lan), ṅo-spród (ngo sprod), gros _débs-pa (gros 'debs pa), to answer, to explain, to advise; ysól-ba _débs-pa (gsol ba 'debs pa) to make a request, smóṅ-lam _débs-pa (smon lam 'debs pa) to offer up a prayer; ysal-_débs byéd-pa (gsal 'debs byed pa) to remember well Mil.; ysal _débs-su šès-pa (gsal 'debs su shes pa) prob to have a distinct recollection of a thing Glr.; rtsis _débs-pa (rtsis 'debs pa) prob. to cast up an account, to reckon, to compute, dei rtsis-ydáb bdág-la med (de'i rtsis gdab bdag la med) I do not take that into account Mil.; lús-la yzér(-gyis) _débs-pa (lus la gzer(-gyis) 'debs pa) Dzl., *zér tab-če (gzer btab ce), or gyab-če* (rgyab ce) W., knocking nails into the body; rgyas _débs-pa (rgyas 'debs pa) to seal; *lúd tab-če (lud btab ce), or gyáb-če* (rgyab ce) W. to spread dung (on the ground), to manure; čus _débs-pa (chus 'debs pa) to sprinkle with water Dzl.; tsa (tshwa), ša túg-pa-la _débs-pa (sha thug pa la 'debs pa) to put salt, meat, into the soup; sá-bon _débs-pa (sa bon 'debs pa) to sow; gur _débs-pa (gur 'debs pa), sga-_débs-pa (sga 'debs pa), to pitch a tent, a camp (driving in the tent-pins); also without a sbst.: sṅar btab-pai ču-gšoṅ-du (sngar btab pa'i chu gshong du) (pitching) in the same dell where they had encamped before Dzl. 272, 1. (Sch. incorr.): hence in general: to found, to establish, e.g. a monastery, frq.; dus _débs-pa (dus 'debs pa) to fix a time.
for mdo (mdo) 3., Cs. _do-yód ('do yod) prudent, clever, _do-méd ('do med) Lex., Cs. imprudent, silly.

1. sbst. Sch.: 'a breed of fine horses'; one Lex. has _do-rta ('do rta) w. e. — 2. vb. Cs.: = zló-ba (bzlo ba), to say, to repeat; ma-_dos-par (ma 'dos par) unspeakable (?) Dzl. 2=V, 4 (the reading of Sch. dubious, v. Schf.'s remarks on this passage).
prob. an incorr. reading for _dógs-pa ('dogs pa).

pf. btags (btags) (also ydags (gdags)!), fut. ydag(s) (gdag(s)), imp. t'ogs (thogs), W. *tag-če* (btag ce); imp *toy (thog) or tag toṅ* (btag tong), 1. to bind, fasten, tie to, (opp. to _gról-ba ('grol ba)), W. *kyi tág-te bor* (khyi btag te bor), tie up, fasten, the dog well; (v. _bór-ba ('bor ba)); la (la) to a thing, frq.; also in a more general sense: to fix, to attach, e.g. a balcony to a house S.g.; to tie round, to buckle on, go-mtsón lús-la (go mtshon lus la) the armour Pth.; to <
